name: Akka
summary: Production actor framework on the JVM with hierarchical supervision trees, typed Behavior[T] mailboxes (since 2.6), Cluster Sharding for location-transparent millions-of-entities deployments, and Akka Persistence for event sourcing; licensed under BSL since 2022 with Apache Pekko as the Apache-licensed fork.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content: Lightbend's **Akka** (Scala/Java, Jonas Bonér et al., 2009+) is the dominant production actor system on managed runtimes. Each Akka actor is a lightweight object with a typed mailbox; actors are organised hierarchically through a **supervision tree** where a parent decides on each child's failure (restart, resume, stop, escalate). Akka's **Typed API** (since 2.6, 2019) enforces compile-time message-type contracts via `Behavior[T]`; the older untyped API exchanged untyped `ActorRef`s that accept `Any`.

sections.title: Contrast with BEAM and Pony
sections.content:
Distinct from BEAM actors (`data/beam-bytecode`, Erlang processes): Akka runs on the JVM with shared-heap memory, so actor isolation is by convention — actors are encouraged to keep state private but the language does not enforce it. Distinct from Pony (`data/pony`): no reference-capability discipline statically prevents data races across actor boundaries.

The compromise is that Akka inherits the JVM's GC, JIT, and ecosystem in exchange for requiring programmer discipline for race-freedom and immutability. For teams already on the JVM, this is often the practical winning trade-off; for green-field designs that want static data-race freedom, Pony or a Rust + actor library is a stronger choice.

sections.title: Akka Cluster and Akka Persistence
sections.content:
**Akka Cluster** adds distribution: actors form a peer-to-peer cluster with gossip-based membership, leader election via Cluster Singleton, partitioning via **Cluster Sharding** (location-transparent actor names sharded by key over consistent hashing), and at-most/at-least/effectively-once delivery via **Akka Persistence** (event sourcing) and durable mailboxes. Cluster Sharding is the load-bearing primitive that lets a typical Akka deployment treat "millions of independent stateful entities" as a routing problem rather than a per-node-state problem.

**Akka Streams** layers reactive streams over the Akka actor system, with materialisation to a graph-of-actors execution — covered as one of the major Reactive Streams implementations in `data/channels#reactive-streams`.

sections.title: Licensing and the Pekko fork
sections.content:
Status (as of 2026-04): production at scale — Twitter/X infrastructure, PayPal, eBay, Norwegian Cruise Line, several telecom platforms.

Akka 2.7+ moved to a Business Source License from Apache 2.0 in 2022, prompting **Apache Pekko** (Apache Incubator) to fork the last Apache-licensed release. The licensing change is the most consequential ecosystem event in the actor space since BEAM's open-source release. New deployments increasingly choose Pekko for its permissive licensing; existing Akka deployments often pay Lightbend for production support.
